Title: A Homogeneous Catalog of Oscillating Solar-Type Stars Observed by 
       the Kepler Mission and a New Amplitude Scaling Relation Including 
       Chromospheric Activity 
Authors: Sayeed M., Huber D., Chontos A., Li Y. 
Table: Stellar properties of 765 solar–like oscillators in our sample
================================================================================
Byte-by-byte Description of file: ajadf648t4_mrt.txt
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   Bytes Format Units     Label   Explanations
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   1-  8 I8     ---       KIC     Kepler Input Catalog identifier
  10- 14 F5.2   mag       Kpmag   Kepler apparent magnitude
  16- 21 F6.1   K         Teff    ? Effective temperature; Berger et al. 2020b
  23- 27 F5.1   K       e_Teff    ? Uncertainty in Teff
  29- 32 F4.2   solRad    Radius  ? Radius (1)
  34- 37 F4.2   solRad  e_Radius  ? Uncertainty in Radius
  39- 42 F4.2   solMass   Mass    ? Mass (1)
  44- 47 F4.2   solMass e_Mass    ? Uncertainty in Mass
  49- 53 F5.2   ---       RUWE    ? Gaia DR3 re–normalized unit weight–error
  55- 59 F5.2   [-]       [Fe/H]  ? Metallicity
  61- 61 I1     ---     r_[Fe/H]  ? Source flag on [Fe/H] (2)
  63- 67 F5.2   d         Period  ? Period
  69- 73 F5.2   d       e_Period  ? Uncertainty in Period
  75- 75 I1     ---     r_Period  ? Source flag on Period (3)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Note (1): Stellar mass and radius were derived with scaling relations 
          (Equations 6 & 7) using pySYD measurements.
Note (2): 
    1 = Serenelli et al. (2017) [2017ApJS..233...23S];
    2 = Buchhave & Latham (2015) [2015ApJ...808..187B]; 
    3 = Bruntt et al. (2012) [2012MNRAS.423..122B].
Note (3):
    1: Santos et al. (2021) [2021ApJS..255...17S];
    2: McQuillan et al. (2014) [2014ApJS..211...24M];
    3: Garcia et al. (2014) [2014A&A...572A..34G].
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
